summary: This week in AI news, OpenAI's GPT-5.6 model has been released, and the company is expanding its reach into households and workplaces. Meanwhile, Meta has faced backlash over its AI-powered features, and Apple has sued OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets. The use of AI in various industries, including healthcare and finance, is also on the rise.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries is leading to a growing need for transparency and accountability, as seen in Google's decision to disclose which ads are made with AI and the controversy surrounding Meta's AI-powered features.
highlights: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 model has been released, promising improvements in cybersecurity and other areas
Meta has faced backlash over its AI-powered features, including the removal of a feature that allowed users to generate AI images based on public Instagram accounts
Apple has sued OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets, highlighting the growing competition in the AI industry

articles.description: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

articles.description: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

articles.title: Humanoid robots controlled by surgeons did world-first operation on live pigs
articles.description: Preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of humanoid robots in surgery.

articles.title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition
articles.description: More intelligence from every token, stronger performance per dollar, and more capability on demand for your hardest work.

articles.title: ChatGPT is now a partner for your most ambitious work
articles.description: ChatGPT Work is an agent that can take action across your apps and files, stay with a project for hours if needed, and turn a goal into finished work.
